description: Fires when all page elements have finished loading (window load event). One of three page-loading triggers: (1) page view – when the browser begins to load the page, (2) DOM ready – when the DOM is ready, (3) page load – when all elements (images, iframes, etc.) have loaded. Use page load when the tag must run after the full page including assets is loaded. Same event conditions as the Page view trigger (Page URL, Page path, Referrer, Campaign, etc.). See Piwik PRO help: Page view trigger.

relationship_to_page_view.note: Three loading options exist: page_view (earliest), dom_ready, page_load (latest). Use page_load when the tag should run only after all page elements have loaded.
relationship_to_page_view.same_conditions: Event condition types and multiplicity options are the same as for the Page view trigger.

alternatives: page_view – Fires when the browser begins to load a page
dom_ready – Fires when HTML and DOM are fully loaded
page_load – Fires when all page elements have finished loading

condition_types.description: Fires when a visitor visits a page with the selected page URL
Fires when a visitor visits a page with the selected page path
Fires when a visitor visits a page on the selected domain
Fires when a visitor was directed by the selected internal referrer
Fires when a visitor was directed by the selected external referrer
Fires when a visitor was directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign)
Fires when traffic source matches (campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct)
Fires when the visitor is a returning visitor
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

multiplicity: Many times during a session, but skips the first event

when_to_use: Run tags only after the full page including images and iframes has loaded
Scripts that depend on layout or dimensions that need all assets loaded
When you need the latest possible moment in the load cycle (after DOM ready)
common_use_cases: Tracking that requires all content to be loaded
Tags that measure or depend on images/iframes
Conversion or analytics that should run after full load
best_practices: Use page_load when the tag depends on images or other subresources; otherwise dom_ready or page_view may be faster
Same URL/referrer condition patterns as page view; add conditions to limit when the trigger fires

create_trigger.description: Create a page load trigger. Fires when all page elements have loaded; same condition structure as page view.

trigger_type.description: Must be 'page_load' for page load triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects; same as page view (Page URL, Page path, Referrer, etc.). Optional.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing page load trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Same as page view: Page URL, Page path, Page hostname, Referrer, External referrer, Campaign, Traffic source, Returning visitor, or variable-based. Optional.
conditions.recommendation: Include at least one URL-based condition to control when the trigger fires.

page_load_all_pages_example.description: Fire on full page load for all pages

page_load_thank_you_example.description: Fire on page load only when Page path contains /thank-you/

common_mistakes: Using page_load when DOM ready or page view would be enough (delays tag execution)
Using page_load on pages with slow or failing assets (tag may fire very late or not at all)

possible_causes: Page load event never fires (e.g. hanging resource)
debugging_steps: Check for broken images or resources that prevent load
